name: GWS CLI Shared Patterns
description: gws CLI: Shared patterns for Google Workspace authentication (OAuth, service accounts), global flags, CLI syntax, output formatting, and security rules. Prerequisite for all GWS skills.

covenant_rules: Never output secrets (API keys, tokens) directly — always sanitize before logging.
Always confirm with the user before executing write/delete commands on Google Workspace resources.
Prefer --dry-run flag for destructive operations to validate locally before calling the API.
Use --sanitize flag for PII/content safety screening when handling sensitive data.

triad_templates.left_hemisphere: Evaluate auth status, flag configuration, and security constraints for the gws CLI invocation: {query}. Verify zero token leaks or unsafe flag combinations exist.
triad_templates.right_hemisphere: Design optimal gws CLI command structures, flag combinations, and output format selections for: {query}. Prefer table or JSON output as appropriate.
triad_templates.main_hemisphere: Coordinate gws CLI setup. Check gws binary availability on PATH, verify authentication state, determine required global flags, construct the command, enforce security rules, and execute: {query}.

steps.name: Verify gws CLI Installation
steps.action: Check that the gws binary is available on PATH and determine the installed version. gws --version must succeed.

steps.name: Check Authentication State
steps.action: Run gws auth login or verify GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS environment variable for service account auth. Ensure valid OAuth or service account credentials are in place.

steps.name: Construct gws Command with Flags
steps.action: Build the final gws <service> <resource> <method> command with appropriate --format, --params, --json, --dry-run, --sanitize, --page-all flags based on the user's intent and method requirements.

steps.name: Confirm and Execute Command
steps.action: Display the constructed command to the user for confirmation on write/delete operations, then execute it. For read-only operations, execute directly with appropriate output format.

steps.name: Format and Present Output
steps.action: Present the CLI output to the user in a readable format. For JSON output, parse and display key fields. For table output, display as formatted table. Never output raw secrets or tokens.

steps.name: GWS Operation Completed
steps.action: Confirm the operation result to the user. If errors occurred, suggest remediation steps such as re-authentication or flag corrections.
